patch.value: Repeated `pm update <id> --blocked-by <other>` calls each REPLACE the stored blocked_by value and the corresponding dependencies edge instead of accumulating, so an agent wiring multiple blockers one call at a time silently loses every blocker except the last. No warning is emitted. Workaround: `--dep "id=<id>,kind=blocked_by"` appends correctly; --blocked-by does not. Impact: dependency chains built incrementally (a very common agent planning pattern) silently degrade to a single edge, corrupting scheduling (pm next) and blocked-status semantics. Fix direction (pick one, keep contracts/docs/completions in sync per the new-flag checklist): (a) comma-list + accumulate semantics matching other comma-list flags, (b) append-by-default with an explicit --set-blocked-by override, or (c) additive --add-blocked-by/--remove-blocked-by pair mirroring --add-tags/--remove-tags. Related shipped history: pm-orrl (create --blocked-by stored free text), pm-kyd6 (update --blocked-by created no deps edge) — this is the third distinct defect on the same flag family; consider a regression spec matrix over create+update x first-set/append/remove. Source: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/484
GH-484: pm update --blocked-by silently overwrites prior blockers instead of appending

Repeated --blocked-by calls accumulate (or the flag rejects a second value with an actionable additive-flag hint); dependencies array and blocked_by field stay consistent; behavior identical via CLI, SDK, and MCP; contracts/docs/completions updated; regression spec covers first-set, append, duplicate, and remove paths for create and update

author: claude-code-agent
message: Start GH-484 blocked-by append semantics implementation

value.note: Append resolved blocked_by dependency edges instead of replacing prior blockers
Expose additive blocked_by edge semantics in SDK command contracts
Regression coverage for append,dedupe,clear blocked_by dependency semantics
Document latest scalar plus full blocker graph behavior

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implementation evidence: update --blocked-by now preserves prior resolved blocked_by dependency edges, deduplicates repeated blockers, and clears all blocker edges only on --unset blocked-by; focused update regression tests passed.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Manual proof: in isolated temp PM_PATH, dist CLI create --blocked-by first then update --blocked-by second kept scalar blocked_by as the second blocker and produced two blocked_by graph edges in pm deps.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Verification: update --blocked-by now preserves existing blocked_by dependency edges while appending resolved new blockers and deduplicating repeats; focused update-command tests passed, manual temp PM_PATH proof showed two blocker edges and latest scalar blocker, full coverage passed 5480 tests at 100/100/100/100, and the CI static sequence passed.

patch.value: Fixed GH-484 by preserving the full blocker dependency graph while keeping blocked_by scalar compatibility; verified with focused tests, manual temp PM_PATH proof, full coverage, and CI static gates.
